title: Identify competitors for DA Agency LLC
body:
**Goal** â€” Produce a documented list of direct and adjacent competitors for DA Agency LLC (https://www.daagencyllc.com/) that the brand can use for positioning and strategy.

**Approach**
- Review the DA Agency LLC website to determine services offered, target industries, and brand positioning.
- Search for agencies with similar service mixes, target markets, and geographic focus.
- Categorize competitors into direct (same services + audience) and adjacent (overlapping services or audience).
- Capture key competitor details: name, website, primary services, target industries, differentiators, and estimated size/signal (e.g., client logos, team page, case studies).
- Summarize findings in a structured comparison table and a short strategic takeaway section.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] At least 8â€“12 relevant competitors identified and documented.
- [ ] Each competitor entry includes: name, website, services, target industries, and a brief note on how they compare to DA Agency LLC.
- [ ] Competitors are labeled as "Direct" or "Adjacent" with rationale.
- [ ] A 1â€“2 paragraph summary highlights 2â€“3 key positioning opportunities or threats for DA Agency LLC.
- [ ] Sources (URLs) are cited for all claims.

**Out of scope**
- No paid tools or subscriptions required; use publicly available information only.
- No outreach to competitors or their clients.
- No redesign of DA Agency LLC's website or brand identity.

title: Research DA Agency LLC brand and services
body: Visit https://www.daagencyllc.com/ and thoroughly catalog what DA Agency LLC does. Extract: company name variations, tagline/slogan, primary services offered, target industries, geographic focus, pricing signals, team size claims, key differentiators they claim, and any client logos/names shown. Also check for linked social profiles (LinkedIn, Instagram, Twitter/X, Facebook). Save all findings as structured notes. This is the baseline to compare competitors against.

title: Find direct competitor agencies by service category
body: Using the brand research (parent task), identify 8-15 direct competitors to DA Agency LLC. Search for agencies offering the same core services in the same geographic market and/or targeting the same client segments. For each competitor found, capture: company name, website URL, primary services, target market, and one key differentiator. Focus on agencies that would appear in the same client consideration set. Use search queries like 'digital marketing agency [city]', '[service type] agency near me', and industry directory listings. Return as a structured list with comparison dimensions.

title: Analyze competitor positioning and market gaps
body: Synthesize the competitor list from parent task into a strategic competitive map. Group competitors by positioning (e.g., premium/full-service, boutique/specialized, budget/transactional). Identify where DA Agency LLC sits relative to these clusters. Highlight 2-3 market gaps or differentiation opportunities DA Agency LLC could exploit. Also flag 2-3 competitors that pose the most direct threat based on service overlap and target similarity. Output: competitive positioning matrix + strategic recommendations.

title: Plan Instagram content strategy for @daautonomous
body:
**Goal** â€” Deliver a documented 30-day content strategy for the @daautonomous Instagram account that grows engagement and clarifies brand positioning.

**Approach**
- Audit the current Instagram profile (bio, feed aesthetics, recent posts, follower count, engagement rates) to establish baseline.
- Research 3-5 comparable accounts in the autonomous systems / AI / tech creator space to identify content patterns that perform well.
- Define 2-3 content pillars (e.g., education, behind-the-scenes, community) aligned with the "daautonomous" brand identity.
- Create a 30-day content calendar with post types, captions, and posting frequency.
- Recommend 3-5 growth tactics (hashtags, collaborations, Reels vs. static) with rationale.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Profile audit summary with strengths, weaknesses, and 3 quick wins.
- [ ] Competitor benchmark table with account names, follower ranges, and top-performing content formats.
- [ ] Documented content pillars with descriptions and example post ideas for each.
- [ ] 30-day content calendar in table format (dates, post type, topic, caption hook).
- [ ] Growth tactics list with expected effort and impact (high/medium/low).
- [ ] All deliverables in a single shareable document (Markdown or Google Doc link).

**Out of scope**
- No actual content creation or posting to the account.
- No paid advertising strategy or budget allocation.
- No cross-platform strategy (TikTok, YouTube, etc.).

title: Audit Instagram account and niche landscape
body: Visit https://www.instagram.com/daautonomous/ and analyze the existing profile, recent posts, visual style, captions, hashtags, follower engagement, and bio positioning. Also research 5-10 comparable accounts in the same niche to identify content gaps, trending formats, posting frequencies, and engagement tactics. Deliver a concise audit report with: (1) current account strengths/weaknesses, (2) competitor insights, (3) recommended content pillars, (4) target audience hypothesis. No posting or automation required.

title: Draft 1-page content strategy: pillars, formats, and weekly cadence for @daautonomous
body:
Using the account audit and niche research outputs, create a 1-page content strategy document for @daautonomous.

Deliverables:
1. Define 3-5 content pillars. For each pillar include:
   - Pillar name and topic focus
   - Purpose mapped to educate / entertain / engage / convert
   - 2-3 example post types (e.g., carousel, Reel, static image, Story series, meme, poll)
2. Recommend a weekly posting cadence:
   - Total posts per week
   - Content mix ratio by pillar and format
   - Ideal posting days/times with timezone
   - Repeatable weekly template (e.g., Monday: educational carousel, Wednesday: Reel, Friday: engagement Story series)
3. Format as a clean, client-ready 1-page strategy doc suitable for handing off to a content-calendar creator.

Acceptance criteria:
- Exactly one output document, concise and scannable
- All 3-5 pillars have topic, purpose, and example formats
- Cadence includes weekly count, mix ratios, days/times, and a reusable template
- Doc is ready to use as input for building a content calendar

title: Draft 2-week content calendar with captions and hooks
body: Create a detailed 2-week content calendar for @daautonomous using the approved content pillars and cadence. For each post include: date, format, pillar, hook/caption draft (with hashtags), visual direction, and CTA. Aim for 8-12 posts total across the two weeks. Deliver as a structured document or spreadsheet-ready format. The calendar should be immediately actionable for creation/scheduling.

title: Propose growth and engagement tactics
body: Develop a shortlist of practical growth and engagement tactics tailored to the @daautonomous niche and content strategy. Include: Story engagement ideas, collaboration/partnership approaches, hashtag strategy, community interaction rules, and 1-2 low-budget promotion options. Keep recommendations realistic and executable without paid tools unless justified. Deliver as a bullet-point action plan.

title: search for oportunities for my company DA Agency
Research RFP and contract opportunities for DA Agency LLC
body:
Research concrete, actionable RFP and contract opportunities for DA Agency LLC (small digital agency offering: web development with Next.js/Vercel, AI workflow automation, video annotation/QA services via SuperAnnotate, and corporate intelligence).

Focus areas:
1. SAM.gov â€” federal contracts under $150K with small business set-asides. Find open solicitations with NAICS codes relevant to web dev (541511, 541512), AI/data services (541519, 541990), and professional services. Include solicitation number, agency, due date, and direct SAM.gov URL.
2. State/local government procurement portals â€” search for active RFPs for web/digital services, IT consulting, or AI/automation. Include portal URL and RFP details.
3. Upwork/LinkedIn â€” enterprise clients actively seeking AI automation partners, web dev agencies, or data annotation vendors. Include client name, project description, and how to apply/contact.
4. Recent news (2025-2026) on AI data labeling contract awards â€” companies that won contracts, contract values, and who to contact for subcontracting opportunities.

Return structured findings with source URLs, contact info, and application instructions where available. Save results to a markdown file in the workspace.

title: Audit @daautonomous Instagram profile and document baseline
body: Perform a comprehensive audit of the @daautonomous Instagram account. Document: (1) Bio analysis â€” clarity, CTA, link usage, brand voice; (2) Feed aesthetics â€” visual consistency, color palette, image quality, grid layout; (3) Recent 12 posts â€” engagement rates (likes + comments + shares / follower count), posting frequency, content types (carousel, Reel, static), caption style; (4) Follower count and growth trajectory if visible; (5) Story highlights and their purpose. Deliver a summary with: 3 clear strengths, 3 weaknesses, and 3 'quick wins' that could be implemented immediately. Format as structured Markdown. No recommendations beyond the quick wins â€” deeper strategy comes later.

title: Research competitor accounts in autonomous systems / AI creator space
body: Identify and analyze 3-5 Instagram accounts comparable to @daautonomous in the autonomous systems, AI, robotics, or tech creator space. For each competitor document: account handle, follower range, content posting frequency, top 3 performing content formats (Reels, carousels, static, Stories), visual style, caption patterns, hashtag strategy, and any unique engagement tactics (e.g., polls, Q&As, collabs). Build a comparison table and highlight 5 content patterns that consistently drive high engagement. Focus on accounts with 10K-500K followers â€” aspirational but achievable. Deliver as structured Markdown with the benchmark table.

title: Define content pillars aligned with daautonomous brand identity
body: Using the profile audit and competitor research (provided as inputs), define 2-3 content pillars for @daautonomous that clarify brand positioning and differentiate from competitors. For each pillar provide: (1) Name and one-sentence definition; (2) Strategic purpose â€” how it serves engagement vs. brand clarity; (3) Target audience segment; (4) 3 example post ideas with format (Reel, carousel, static) and caption hook; (5) Visual direction notes. Ensure pillars feel authentic to 'autonomous systems' expertise while being accessible to Instagram's general audience. Avoid generic pillars â€” they should be distinctive to this brand. Deliver as structured Markdown.

title: Build 30-day content calendar with post types and captions
body: Create a detailed 30-day content calendar for @daautonomous using the approved content pillars. For each day specify: date, content pillar, post type (Reel / carousel / static / Story series), topic/title, caption hook (first 2 lines designed to stop scroll), and suggested posting time. Distribute posts across pillars strategically â€” don't repeat same pillar 3+ days in a row. Include 2-3 'flex' slots for timely/reactive content. Ensure 40% Reels, 35% carousels, 25% static as a starting mix. Every caption hook should feel native to Instagram, not corporate. Deliver as a clean Markdown table plus a narrative walkthrough of weekly themes.

title: Recommend 3-5 growth tactics with effort and impact ratings
body: Develop 3-5 specific, actionable growth tactics for @daautonomous tailored to the autonomous systems/AI niche on Instagram. For each tactic provide: name, detailed implementation steps, expected effort (high/medium/low), expected impact (high/medium/low), time to see results, and rationale based on competitor research or platform trends. Tactics must cover: (1) hashtag strategy â€” specific hashtag tiers and examples; (2) collaboration approach â€” who to partner with and how to pitch; (3) Reels vs. static optimization â€” when to use each; plus 2 additional tactics of your choice. Avoid generic advice like 'post consistently.' Be specific about what makes each tactic right for THIS account. Deliver as structured Markdown with a summary matrix.

title: Compile all deliverables into single shareable strategy document
body: Assemble the complete @daautonomous Instagram Content Strategy into one polished, shareable document. Integrate: profile audit summary, competitor benchmark table, content pillars with examples, 30-day content calendar, and growth tactics with impact ratings. Add: executive summary (1 paragraph), implementation timeline (what to do in weeks 1-2 vs. 3-4), and a 'start here' checklist of 5 immediate actions. Ensure consistent formatting, clear headers, and professional but brand-appropriate tone. Output as clean Markdown suitable for conversion to Google Doc or Notion. Include table of contents with anchor links. The document should be ready to present to stakeholders without additional editing.

title: Map content pillars to 2-week posting schedule
body: Create the structural backbone for @daautonomous's 2-week calendar. Inputs: approved content pillars and cadence (assumed available in project docs or infer from brand). Output: A dated schedule mapping 8-12 post slots to specific dates, formats (carousel, reel, static, story, etc.), and assigned pillars. Ensure variety in format and pillar distribution across the two weeks. Include recommended posting times. Deliver as a simple table or list with columns: Date | Day | Format | Pillar | Notes. This is the skeleton; no captions yet.

title: Draft hooks and captions for all scheduled posts
body: Write complete hook/caption copy for all 8-12 posts in the approved schedule. Each post needs: (1) an attention-grabbing opening hook (first 1-2 lines), (2) full caption body with brand voice, (3) 3-5 relevant hashtags, (4) clear CTA. Match tone to @daautonomous brand (autonomous systems, tech-forward, likely professional-edgy). Vary hook styles: question, bold statement, data point, pain point, curiosity gap. Label each with its post date/pillar for assembly. Deliver as a document with clear post identifiers.

title: Define visual direction for each post
body: Create visual briefs for all 8-12 scheduled posts. For each post specify: visual format (e.g., 4:5 carousel, 9:16 reel cover, single graphic), color palette reference, imagery style (photography, 3D render, UI screenshot, illustration), key visual elements, text overlay requirements, and reference/mood notes. Align with @daautonomous brand aesthetic. Cross-reference with assigned pillar and format from the schedule. Deliver as structured briefs tied to post dates.

title: Assemble final calendar in spreadsheet-ready format
body: Combine all components into a single, immediately actionable deliverable. Merge: schedule structure (from task 0), hooks/captions/CTAs/hashtags (from task 1), and visual directions (from task 2) into one structured document. Format as CSV-ready or clean table with columns: Date | Day | Format | Pillar | Hook (full) | Caption Body | Hashtags | CTA | Visual Direction Summary | Status (Ready to Create / Needs Asset). Include a brief intro with posting cadence summary and any scheduling notes. Export-friendly for Google Sheets, Notion, or Later/Hootsuite import.

title: Profile DA Agency LLC services and positioning
body: Visit https://www.daagencyllc.com/ and thoroughly document: (1) all services offered with descriptions, (2) target industries or client types mentioned, (3) geographic focus or markets served, (4) brand messaging and positioning claims, (5) any client logos, case studies, or team size signals. Also check their LinkedIn, Crunchbase, or other public profiles for additional context. Output a structured summary with exact quotes and page URLs. This profile will be used as the benchmark for competitor matching.

title: Research direct competitors for DA Agency LLC
body: Find 4-6 direct competitors to DA Agency LLC â€” agencies offering the same or very similar services to the same target industries and audience. Use Google searches, industry directories (Clutch, Agency Spotter, DesignRush), and LinkedIn. For each competitor, capture: name, website URL, primary services list, target industries, geographic focus, key differentiators, estimated size/signal (team count, client logos, notable case studies), and a brief note on how closely they match DA Agency. Label each as "Direct" with rationale. Cite all sources with URLs. Aim for competitors of comparable or slightly larger scale, not massive holding companies unless they have a directly competing division.

title: Research adjacent competitors for DA Agency LLC
body: Find 4-6 adjacent competitors to DA Agency LLC â€” agencies with overlapping services OR overlapping target audience but not both identical. Examples: agencies serving same industries with different service mix, or offering similar services to different but related markets. Use the same research methods as direct competitor task. For each, capture: name, website URL, primary services, target industries, geographic focus, key differentiators, estimated size/signal, and brief note on the overlap and gap vs. DA Agency. Label each as "Adjacent" with rationale explaining the partial overlap. Cite all sources with URLs.

title: Synthesize competitor analysis and strategic takeaways
body: Combine outputs from the two research tasks into a final deliverable. Create: (1) a structured comparison table with all 8-12 competitors showing name, website, type (Direct/Adjacent), services, target industries, size/signal, and comparison note; (2) a 1-2 paragraph strategic summary highlighting 2-3 key positioning opportunities or threats for DA Agency LLC based on gaps, crowded spaces, or differentiation chances you observe; (3) a complete source list with all URLs cited. Ensure the table is scannable and the strategic takeaways are actionable for brand positioning decisions. Format in clean Markdown.

title: Produce content: Watch One Operator Run a Whole Agency
body:
Platform: tiktok · Format: reel
Hook: One directive. Watch six agents spawn, route, and ship — no team, no per-seat bill.
Why now: Ties to 'How to setup a local coding agent on macOS' (HN 412) — local agent curiosity is spiking; we show the multi-agent version, not the single-agent toy.
Pattern source: Remixes the demo-reel format from @rourke/@drcintas — screen-capture build-in-public with a 'Comment FLEET' deliverable

Deliverable: final caption + script/copy ready to post, in the DA Agency / Agent Legion brand voice (see BRAND_STRATEGY.md).
